exemplar.title: Finding the Volume and Surface Area Change of Mount Saint Helens caused by its eruption in 1980

detailedExemplarData.title: Finding the Volume and Surface Area Change of Mount Saint Helens caused by its eruption in 1980

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, logical narrative with contextual introduction, modelling, calculation, evaluation and conclusion
Well-defined headings and numbered sections that guide the reader
Figures and tables placed close to references enhance readability
Consistently formatted bibliography and clear descriptive title
weaknesses: Minor digressions and some lengthy explanations that slightly impede conciseness
Incomplete captions on a few figures and inconsistent styling of superscripts
Omission of units (e.g., mÂ³, kmÂ³) in some final results
Blurry images reduce clarity in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration exhibits a coherent and logically developed structure with a clear introduction, body and conclusion, satisfying the band-4 descriptors for coherence and organization. While there are a few presentation lapses (incomplete captions, minor digressions, omitted units), these do not undermine the overall excellence of the structure and relevance of the content, justifying the top mark of 4.

strengths: Mostly correct and appropriate use of integrals, limits, piece-wise notation and logarithms
Effective multiple representations: coordinate tables, 2-D graphs, 3-D solids and numerical summaries
Logical flow of mathematical reasoning with explanations accompanying each major step
weaknesses: Occasional unit inconsistencies (kmÂ² vs. kmÂ³) and unclear unit notation in tables
Blurred images and hard-to-read axes in some figures
Typographical errors (misplaced range limits) that slightly impede clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is generally clear and logically structured with mostly consistent notation and effective representations, matching the band-3 descriptors. The minor lapses in unit consistency and figure clarity prevent a band-4 award, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Personal connection to a Czech background demonstrates genuine engagement
Evidence of independent thinking in selecting a real volcanic event and custom modelling choices
Use of image rotation, scaling and best-fit testing shows creativity and initiative
weaknesses: Mathematical methods remain within standard SL repertoire
Approach is good but not highly innovative or exceptional
Creativity and initiative are present but not sustained at an outstanding level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows clear personal engagement through contextual motivation and independent choices, aligning with band-2 descriptors. While creative and personal, the methods do not reach the outstanding innovation required for band-3, so the score remains at 2.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on numerical results, relating volume loss and surface-area change to the investigationâs aim
Thoughtful discussion of sources of error (symmetry assumption, scaling uncertainties, camera angles)
Clear evaluation of outcomes with identification of strengths and weaknesses of methods
weaknesses: Reflection remains qualitative with no quantitative error analysis
Recommendations for future improvements are only briefly mentioned
Depth of critical analysis is competent but not substantial
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of limitations, fitting the band-2 descriptors. However, the lack of quantitative error discussion and only brief future considerations keep the reflection from reaching band-3, justifying a score of 2.

strengths: Effective and comprehensive use of integral calculus (disc and surface-of-revolution methods) and logarithmic/polynomial modelling
Appropriate selection and justification of models using RÂ² criteria
Piece-wise definitions and unit conversions applied correctly to support the aim
Mathematical techniques fully commensurate with SL level and applied across the investigation
weaknesses: Minor arithmetic slip in the crater integral and inconsistent integration limits
Typographical errors in unit exponents (kmÂ² vs. kmÂ³) and domain notation overlaps
Lack of explicit demonstration of intermediate steps in some derivations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates comprehensive use of relevant SL mathematicsâcalculus, modelling and statistical criteriaâapplied effectively throughout. Despite a few inaccuracies and omissions, the mathematics reaches band-5 standards for comprehensive application, warranting a score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany every major step, and the order in which mathematical processes are presented mirrors the logical derivation. Occasional typographical errors (e.g., misplaced range limits) slightly impede clarity, but overall the argument remains understandable and logically structured.

Derivations and integrations show good conceptual grasp, and most numerical work is sensible. Nevertheless, unit-label inconsistencies (kmÂ² vs kmÂ³) and a probable arithmetic slip in the crater integral reveal minor inaccuracies, preventing a higher score that requires mostly or completely correct work.

The student employs coordinate tables, 2-D graphs, 3-D solids, and numerical summaries to illustrate the model. These representations meaningfully complement one another and help the reader understand the procedure. A few images are blurred and axes are sometimes hard to read, so the use is effective rather than highly effective.

The investigation follows a clear narrative: contextual introduction, modelling, calculation, comparison, evaluation and conclusion. Each section builds on the previous one without abrupt jumps, and cross-referencing of figures and tables supports the logical flow. Minor digressions (long explanations of basic ideas already introduced) prevent the work from being considered âhighly coherentâ, but overall the development is consistently logical.

Using image rotation, scaling, best-fit testing and a piece-wise function indicates creativity and initiative beyond simply copying textbook procedures, but the approach stays within the expected repertoire for this topic, so the level is good rather than exceptional.

Section 8 discusses several sources of errorâsymmetry assumption, scaling uncertainties, different camera anglesâshowing thoughtful analysis of limitations, but the discussion remains qualitative and does not, for example, estimate error bounds, so insight is competent rather than deep.

Outcomes are evaluated against expectations, and strengths and weaknesses of the method are identified. Future improvements are mentioned only briefly, so the evaluation is clear but not comprehensive.

The student comments on what the numerical results mean (percentage loss of volume, increase in surface area) and links these to the investigationâs goal, providing meaningful but not deeply critical reflection.

Integrals, limits, piece-wise notation and logarithms are used correctly in most places, showing good control of mathematical language. Isolated slipsâsuch as mixing kmÂ² and kmÂ³ in one sentence and inconsistent variable namingâindicate that accuracy is not flawless, so the communication fits the âmostly consistentâ rather than the âfully consistentâ descriptor.

The exploration is clearly framed around the studentâs personal curiosityâshe links the task to her Czech background and justifies methodological decisions in her own wordsâshowing a genuine personal approach, albeit not an innovative one.

Most material directly supports the aim of estimating volume and surface-area change. Some repetition occurs when explaining scaling and unit conversions, and lengthy quotations of standard formulas add bulk. Consequently the text is generally concise and relevant but not uniformly so.

Integral calculus (disc and surface-of-revolution methods), logarithmic and polynomial modelling, RÂ² comparison and unit conversions are all directly relevant to the stated aim and are used effectively to reach results. The mathematics is more than adequate, though not yet comprehensive or expert level.

Selecting a real volcanic event, extracting data from photographs, creating a piece-wise model and choosing fit criteria (RÂ²) demonstrate noticeable independent choices beyond classroom examples, though the mathematical techniques themselves remain fairly standard.

Headings, numbered sections (Introduction, Modelling, Calculation, Evaluation, Bibliography) and sub-sections give the exploration a recognisable structure, making it easy to follow. Figures are placed close to the text that references them. However, a few captions are incomplete and the placement of long tables occasionally breaks the visual rhythm, so the organisation is solid rather than exceptional.

The work employs calculus and modelling that sit solidly within the SL syllabus, with piece-wise definition and integration by parts adding complexity. Hence the mathematics is fully commensurate with course level but does not venture beyond it.

annotations.comment: The volume range for f(x) is inconsistent (0â195 here vs. earlier 0â220); ensure the integration limits match the defined domains.
The title is clear and descriptive, immediately conveying the focus of the exploration.
Consider adding a concise statement of the investigationâs aim on the title page to help orient the reader from the outset.
The first paragraph effectively situates the eruption historically, but its length affects conciseness; consider breaking it into two shorter paragraphs.
The connection to your Czech background is a strong personal approach, demonstrating genuine engagement with the topic.
Break long sentences into shorter ones and reduce repetition to improve flow and reader engagement.
Including a large dataset of 15 coordinate points is commendable; it provides a robust foundation for modelling.
The unit notation â(10m1s)â is unclear; standardize and clarify the units for both axes in the table header.
Label the table axes clearly and include units consistently under âPoint,â âx-coordinate,â and ây-coordinate.â
Figure 1 has a descriptive caption, but the axes on the image itself are hard to read; consider annotating axis lines directly on the figure.
The blurred quality of the rotated image reduces clarity; using a higher-resolution screenshot would enhance readability.
Defining the x-axis as height and y-axis as width is clear; this use of multiple representations supports understanding.
Reporting the polynomial fit's RÂ² value of 0.994 demonstrates effective use of statistical criteria to justify model choice.
When introducing f(x), explicitly state the method or software used to derive these coefficients to strengthen mathematical transparency.
The limits â0 â¤ x â¤ 295â should use proper mathematical notation (with braces) for clarity and consistency.
The integral expression for volume is correctly applied; including the general disc method formula reinforces mathematical communication.
Units are omitted in the final volume result; always include âmÂ³â or âkmÂ³â to avoid ambiguity.
Include a brief note on the impact of the scale factor and why volume scales with factor 10Â³ due to linear scaling of dimensions.
Converting from mÂ³ to kmÂ³ is correctly executed, but an intermediate step showing the division by 10â¹ would aid clarity.
The explanation of combining piecewise functions is clear, but revise the domain notation to avoid overlapping intervals.
The selected logarithmic fit g(x) yields a high RÂ² (0.987), indicating a strong piece for the crater model.
When constructing h(x), explicitly state the rationale for overlapping domains and resolve the interval inconsistency
The step converting 1.388Ã10Â¹Â¹ mÂ³ to kmÂ³ is correct; including the units âkmÂ³â in the final result would strengthen accuracy.
Integration by parts is presented, but the evaluation of the resulting antiderivative at the limits 15 and 195 should be shown explicitly.
There is a unit typo: surface area is reported in âkmÂ³â instead of âkmÂ²â on page 15; please correct the exponent to reflect area.
The derivative gâ²(x) is given as 509/(5x) but should be 101.8/x; verify the differentiation of the logarithmic function.
Surface area results are presented clearly; adding a brief sensitivity analysis would deepen the reflection on accuracy.
The conclusion succinctly summarizes numerical findings, connecting volume loss to percentage erupted; this enhances reader comprehension.
To deepen reflection, discuss how model assumptions (e.g., symmetry) might quantitatively affect the percentage results.
Bibliography entries are consistently formatted with URLs and access dates, demonstrating good presentation.
Ensure that all superscripts (e.g., volume and area units) are consistently styled in the bibliography and main text.
